title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
summary: Tool calling is the mechanism by which LLM-based agents request and execute external tools to access real-time data and take actions beyond language generation. The agent requests a tool invocation through its system prompt, the framework executes the tool, and the loop continues, fundamentally distinguishing agents from stateless chatbots.
writeup: This video explains tool calling, the mechanism by which LLM-based agents request and execute external tools to access real-time data and take actions beyond language generation. It walks through the loop: the agent requests a tool invocation through its system prompt, the framework executes the tool, and the cycle continues. The central takeaway is that this tool-calling loop is what fundamentally distinguishes agents from stateless chatbots.
